tex:
\begin{question*}{13}{a}Paul has a set of 8 cards, each with a number written on it.\\
The numbers on the cards are 1, 1, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5.\\
One card is taken at random.

Write down the probability that the number on the card is

\begin{subpartsaliged}
1,

an odd number,

a prime number,

a number less than 6.

\end{subpartsaliged}

(b)Dina has a set of 12 cards.\\
These are the numbers on the cards.
\\[0.2cm]
\indent 3 \quad 4 \quad 1 \quad 3 \quad 2 \quad 1 \quad 3 \quad 4 \quad 2 \quad 2 \quad 1 \quad 3

Work out

\begin{subpartsaliged}
the median,

the mode,

the mean,

the range.

\end{subpartsaliged}

(c)Helena has a different set of cards.\\
She takes one card at random and records the number shown.\\
She does this 50 times.\\
The results are shown in the table.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|}
\hline
Number on card & Frequency \\
\hline
1 & 8 \\
2 & 11 \\
3 & 10 \\
4 & 9 \\
5 & 12 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Calculate the mean of her results.

\end{question*}
texHtml:
\begin{question*}{13}{a}Paul has a set of 8 cards, each with a number written on it.\\ The numbers on the cards are 1, 1, 2, 3, 3, 3, 4, 5.\\ One card is taken at random. Write down the probability that the number on the card is \begin{subpartsaliged} 1, an odd number, a prime number, a number less than 6. \end{subpartsaliged} (b)Dina has a set of 12 cards.\\ These are the numbers on the cards. \\[0.2cm] \indent 3 \quad 4 \quad 1 \quad 3 \quad 2 \quad 1 \quad 3 \quad 4 \quad 2 \quad 2 \quad 1 \quad 3 Work out \begin{subpartsaliged} the median, the mode, the mean, the range. \end{subpartsaliged} (c)Helena has a different set of cards.\\ She takes one card at random and records the number shown.\\ She does this 50 times.\\ The results are shown in the table.
Number on cardFrequency
18
211
310
49
512
Calculate the mean of her results. \end{question*}

tex:
\begin{question*}{13}{a}Jeremy goes on holiday.\\
He parks his car in the airport car park from

10 00 on Tuesday 17 July to 17 00 on Saturday 28 July.
The car park charges are shown below.

\begin{tabular}{ll}
Monday to Friday & \$14 per day \\
Saturday and Sunday & \$8 per day \\
\end{tabular}
\textit{Part days are charged as full days}
Find the total cost of parking his car.

(b)At the airport, Jeremy buys a ring for \$53 and a watch for \$65.

Work out how much change he receives from \$120.

(c)The plane flies from Melbourne to Tokyo at an average speed of 783 km/h.\\
The distance from Melbourne to Tokyo is 8352 km.\\
The plane leaves Melbourne at 09 52 local time.\\
The local time in Tokyo is 2 hours behind the local time in Melbourne.

Find the local time in Tokyo when the plane arrives.

(d)In Tokyo, Jeremy buys a bracelet for 2050 yen.\\
The exchange rate is 1 yen = \$0.0125 .

Calculate the price of the bracelet in dollars.\\
Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar.

(e)The plane ticket costs \$680 plus a tax of 16\%.

Find the total cost of this ticket.

\end{question*}
texHtml:
\begin{question*}{13}{a}Jeremy goes on holiday.\\ He parks his car in the airport car park from 10 00 on Tuesday 17 July to 17 00 on Saturday 28 July. The car park charges are shown below.
Monday to Friday\$14 per day
Saturday and Sunday\$8 per day
\textit{Part days are charged as full days} Find the total cost of parking his car. (b)At the airport, Jeremy buys a ring for \$53 and a watch for \$65. Work out how much change he receives from \$120. (c)The plane flies from Melbourne to Tokyo at an average speed of 783 km/h.\\ The distance from Melbourne to Tokyo is 8352 km.\\ The plane leaves Melbourne at 09 52 local time.\\ The local time in Tokyo is 2 hours behind the local time in Melbourne. Find the local time in Tokyo when the plane arrives. (d)In Tokyo, Jeremy buys a bracelet for 2050 yen.\\ The exchange rate is 1 yen = \$0.0125 . Calculate the price of the bracelet in dollars.\\ Give your answer correct to the nearest dollar. (e)The plane ticket costs \$680 plus a tax of 16\%. Find the total cost of this ticket. \end{question*}

tex:
Belle records the height, in centimetres, and the mass, in kilograms, of some goats.\\
Some of her results are shown in the scatter diagram.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) The table shows four more results.

\begin{tabular}{|lcccc|}
\hline
Height (cm) & 23 & 30 & 36 & 38 \\
Mass (kg) & 31.2 & 33.5 & 34.6 & 34.8 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
Plot these points on the scatter diagram.

(b) What type of correlation is shown in this scatter diagram?

(c) (i) (d) Draw a line of best fit on the scatter diagram.

(e) Use your line of best fit to estimate the height of a goat with mass 32.5 kg.

(f) Work out the percentage of the 12 goats that have a height between 26 cm and 35 cm.
texHtml:
Belle records the height, in centimetres, and the mass, in kilograms, of some goats.\\ Some of her results are shown in the scatter diagram. (a) The table shows four more results.
Height (cm)23303638
Mass (kg)31.233.534.634.8
Plot these points on the scatter diagram. (b) What type of correlation is shown in this scatter diagram? (c) (i) (d) Draw a line of best fit on the scatter diagram. (e) Use your line of best fit to estimate the height of a goat with mass 32.5 kg. (f) Work out the percentage of the 12 goats that have a height between 26 cm and 35 cm.

tex:
Alexa, Ben and Chloe own a restaurant.

(a) Alexa records some temperatures.

Fridge 4 \textdegree C \quad Cool box -3 \textdegree C \quad Freezer -19 \textdegree C
(i) (b) Find the difference in temperature between the fridge and the cool box.

(c) Find the difference in temperature between the cool box and the freezer.

(d) The temperature in the cold room is 5 \textdegree C lower than the fridge.

Find the temperature in the cold room.

(e) Alexa, Ben and Chloe share the profits from their restaurant in the ratio 2 : 6 : 7.\\
One year the restaurant makes a profit of \$60 000.

Work out how much each receives.

(f) They invest \$12 000 at a rate of $n$\% per year simple interest.\\
At the end of 3 years the value of the investment is \$12 900.

Find the value of $n$.

(a) $T = 3a^2b$

Find the value of $T$ when $a = 4$ and $b = 5$.

(b) (i) (c) Multiply out the brackets.
\[
x(3 - 5x)
\]

(d) Factorise fully.
\[
5x - 20x^2
\]

(e) \begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

Find an expression for the perimeter of this triangle.\\
Give your answer in its simplest form.

\begin{InsertScreenShot}

\end{InsertScreenShot}

(a) Describe fully the single transformation that maps

(i) (b) triangle $A$ onto triangle $B$,

(c) triangle $A$ onto triangle $C$,

(d) triangle $A$ onto triangle $D$.

(e) On the grid, draw the image of triangle $A$ after a reflection in the line $y = -1$.

(a) Complete the table of values for $y = x^2 - 3x - 6$.

\begin{tabular}{|l|ccccccccccc|}
\hline
$x$ & -3 & -2 & -1 & 0 & 1 & 2 & 3 & 4 & 5 & 6 \\
\hline
$y$ & 12 & ... & -2 & ... & ... & ... & ... & -2 & ... & 12 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(b) On the grid, draw the graph of $y = x^2 - 3x - 6$ for $-3 \leqslant x \leqslant 6$.

(c) Write down the equation of the line of symmetry of the graph.

(d) Use your graph to solve the equation $x^2 - 3x - 6 = 0$.

tex:

(i) (b) $5x = -30$

(c) $4x - 2 = 28$

(d) $3(2x + 7) = 12$

(e) Solve the simultaneous equations.\\
You must show all your working.

\[
\begin{aligned}
5x - 2y &= 44 \\
2x + 3y &= 10
